name: Puntal Blanco - circular desde Fontanar
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Pozo Alcón offer a diverse range of terrain within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features a unique blend of lush mountains, dense forests, and semi-desertic badlands, providing varied backdrops for riding. Riders can expect routes through mountainous areas like Sierra del Pozo with impressive rocky formations, as well as arid landscapes in the Guadiana Menor Geopark. The area is characterized by significant elevation changes and natural features such as reservoirs and waterfalls. Best mountain bike trails around Pozo Alcón The most popular mountain bike trail is Puntal Blanco - circular desde Fontanar, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puente de la Herradura – Fuente de Acero Refuge loop from Hotel Rural Los Nogales, a difficult 31.7 miles (51.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Robustiano the Goblin’s Little House – Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Pozo Alcón, a 19.8 miles (31.8 km) trail leading through natural surroundings with waterfalls, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Pozo Alcón is defined by dense forests, rocky formations, and semi-desertic badlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy gravel paths to highly technical trails. The routes in Pozo Alcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pozo Alcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pozo Alcón?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Pozo Alcón listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. These include routes through lush mountains and semi-desertic badlands.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Pozo Alcón?
A: Mountain biking in Pozo Alcón offers a unique blend of terrain. You'll find everything from easy gravel paths and paved sections to rugged, highly technical mountain trails. The landscape transitions from dense forests and rocky formations in areas like Sierra del Pozo to arid, semi-desertic badlands in the Guadiana Menor Geopark.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Pozo Alcón?
A: Yes, while many routes offer significant challenges, there are options suitable for beginners. The region features easy gravel paths, and some moderate routes can be adapted for less experienced riders. For example, the area around Arroyo del Guazalamanco offers beautiful natural surroundings that can be explored at a more relaxed pace.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the area around Arroyo del Guazalamanco is known for its magnificent natural surroundings and beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robustiano the Goblin’s Little House – Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Pozo Alcón&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through this scenic area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer access to stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can find impressive rocky formations in Sierra del Pozo, and the unique semi-desertic landscapes of the Guadiana Menor Geopark. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark, and the Mirador del Lirio offers spectacular panoramic views, especially towards the 'desert' of Guadiana Menor.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Pozo Alcón are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993791067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal Blanco - circular desde Fontanar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura – Fuente de Acero Refuge loop from Hotel Rural Los Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more challenging circular path.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pozo Alcón?
A: The mountain bike trails in Pozo Alcón are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the variety of options available for different skill levels, from challenging climbs to scenic descents.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Pozo Alcón offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31864579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura – Fuente de Acero Refuge loop from Mirador Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in the Sierra del Pozo area?
A: The Sierra del Pozo, within the natural park, is rich in flora and fauna. While mountain biking, you might spot mountain goats, deer, and various birds, including impressive griffon vultures soaring overhead.

Q: Are there any interesting historical or cultural sites near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Pozo Alcón also has historical and cultural appeal. While specific trails may not pass directly through them, sites such as the Castillo de La Iruela and the Torre del Homenaje are within the broader region and offer cultural points of interest.

Q: Where can I find unique geological formations while mountain biking?
A: The region is home to fascinating geological formations. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of a Geopark and offer unique semi-desertic landscapes and canyons.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Pozo Alcón?
A: Pozo Alcón offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, especially in the more arid areas, while winters are milder but can see cooler temperatures in the mountainous regions.
tips: Summit located on the border between Granada and Jaén, both provinces located in Andalusia. With its more than 870m high, it offers panoramic views of the surrounding desert mountains.

The Negratín reservoir was inaugurated in 1984 and its construction meant the disappearance of the town of Las Juntas and the old spa of Zujar, as well as various farmhouses that remained under the waters of the reservoir.
The Negratín reservoir is the second largest reservoir in the Autonomous Community of Andalusia, with more than 567 Hm3.
